title: Karwinskia humboldtiana
taxonomy: Karwinskia humboldtiana is a flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is native to Mexico. The plant is also known as coyote melon or devil's head cactus, although it is not a cactus. Karwinskia humboldtiana is classified as a shrub or a small tree. Its classification is further divided into the genus Karwinskia and the species K. humboldtiana.

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
